lenses.guidance: Pharaoh's 'Who is the LORD that I should obey his voice?' (v 2) frames the entire Exodus narrative as a question. The structure of the next twelve chapters is the answer Yahweh gives — through plague, sea, and Sinai.

The first confrontation produces deeper bondage, not deliverance (vv 6-9). The redemptive arc bends through intensified suffering before relief — a pattern the prophets and the cross will both later exhibit.
Moses returns to God with 'why have you sent me at all?' (v 22). Pray when faithfulness seems to produce only harm; God's servants are permitted bewildered prayer without losing their commission.
